summary: Epic Dark Star > St. Stephen and tight Eleven suite
blurb: The show delivers a landmark Dark Star into Saint Stephen sequence, followed by a powerful The Eleven and Turn On Your Lovelight. Jerry's soaring leads and Phil's thunderous bass anchor the improvisations, while Tom’s organ weaves through the Cryptical suite.
review: On February 27, 1969 at the Fillmore West the Dead unleashed one of their most legendary mid‑summer runs. The second set is a masterclass in psychedelic storytelling: Mountains Of The Moon feeds into an otherworldly Dark Star that rockets into Saint Stephen and then surges through a blistering Eleven before resolving on Turn On Your Lovelight. The first set’s Cryptical Envelopment → Drums → The Other One → Cryptical Envelopment suite showcases the band’s telepathic interplay, with Tom Constanten’s organ dancing around Jerry Garcia’s cosmic guitar flights and Bill Kreutzmann’s explosive drum breaks. Across all available tapes the performance feels locked‑in – Jerry’s “coasters of red‑white‑blue light” solos, Phil’s thunder‑thrown bass, Bob’s fluid fills, and a dual‑drum engine that pushes the improvisations to new heights. For anyone wanting the definitive experience, the 16‑track soundboard capture (gd69-02-27.sbd.16track.kaplan.6315.sbeok.shnf) offers crystal‑clear mix and balance that even surpasses the Live/Dead master.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings are strongly positive, praising the Dark Star > Saint Stephen sequence, The Eleven, and overall band cohesion; no consistent negative comments appear. This consensus warrants a solid positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent 16‑track soundboard mix, open and clear balance that outshines Live/Dead; captures every instrument with superb separation.

key_highlights: Landmark Dark Star → Saint Stephen sequence with soaring solos
Powerful The Eleven that drives the second set momentum
Cryptical Envelopment → Drums → The Other One suite showcasing tight improvisation
Crystal‑clear 16‑track SBD mix that renders the performance vivid

band_performance.Jerry: Soars on cosmic leads, delivering fiery solos on Dark Star and Lovelight; his tone described as fuzzy yet ear‑piercing, lighting the show with “coasters of red‑white‑blue light.”
band_performance.Phil: Throws down thunder on bass, anchoring the improvisations and creating a maelstrom especially in the second‑set jams.
band_performance.Bob: Provides fluid right‑hand fills and solid rhythmic support; his promise to “bring out the monkey” translates into creative, clear guitar work throughout.
band_performance.Tom: Organ interplay is stellar, weaving seamlessly with Jerry’s leads during the Cryptical suite and adding depth to the entire set.
band_performance.Mickey: Delivers tight, complementary percussion that locks in with Bill’s explosive drumming, enhancing the dynamic flow of the jams.
band_performance.Billy: Powerful drum breaks and a pronounced presence (noted as higher in the mix) drive the energy; his partnership with Mickey creates a thunderous rhythmic foundation.
